root_text.an10.101:1.1: “Tisso imā, bhikkhave, samaṇasaññā bhāvitā bahulīkatā satta dhamme paripūrenti.
root_text.an10.101:1.3: Vevaṇṇiyamhi ajjhupagato, parapaṭibaddhā me jīvikā, añño me ākappo karaṇīyoti—
root_text.an10.101:1.4: imā kho, bhikkhave, tisso samaṇasaññā bhāvitā bahulīkatā satta dhamme paripūrenti.
root_text.an10.101:2.2: Santatakārī hoti santatavutti sīlesu, anabhijjhālu hoti, abyāpajjo hoti, anatimānī hoti, sikkhākāmo hoti, “idamatthan”tissa hoti jīvitaparikkhāresu, āraddhavīriyo ca viharati.
root_text.an10.101:2.3: Imā kho, bhikkhave, tisso samaṇasaññā bhāvitā bahulīkatā ime satta dhamme paripūrentī”ti.

translation_text.an10.101:1.1: “Mendicants, when these three perceptions for ascetics are developed and cultivated they fulfill seven things.
translation_text.an10.101:1.3: ‘I have secured freedom from class.’ ‘My livelihood is tied up with others.’ ‘My behavior should be different.’
translation_text.an10.101:1.4: When these three perceptions for ascetics are developed and cultivated they fulfill seven things.
translation_text.an10.101:2.2: Their deeds and behavior are always consistent with the precepts. They’re content, kind-hearted, and humble. They want to train. They use the necessities of life after reflecting on their purpose. They’re energetic.
translation_text.an10.101:2.3: When those three perceptions for ascetics are developed and cultivated they fulfill these seven things.”

variant_text.an10.101:2.2: Santatakārī → niccaṁ santatakārī (bj); satatakārī (sya-all, pts1ed, mr) | santatavutti → satatavuttī (sya-all, pts1ed) | āraddhavīriyo ca → āraddhaviriyo ca (bj, pts1ed); āraddhaviriyo (sya-all)
